description: plugin.json has no skills array; the plugin's sole artifact skills/adversarial-spec/SKILL.md is not declared in the manifest, breaking auto-discovery

evidence: No example blocks found anywhere in SKILL.md (searched for ## Examples, <example>, and similar markers)
description: Zero example blocks present; skills require at least one worked invocation example showing input and expected output
suggested_fix: Add an ## Examples section with a sample invocation (e.g., user asks to refine a spec file) and the skill's expected output format

evidence: "conduct a comprehensive interview" (line ~200) and "write a comprehensive spec" (line ~263)
description: 'comprehensive' used twice as a vague quantifier; replace with concrete criteria such as 'covers all 8 interview topic categories' or 'all required sections populated'
suggested_fix: Replace both instances of 'comprehensive' with measurable criteria referencing the 8 interview topic categories listed in Step 0.5

evidence: "it's a thorough requirements gathering session" at line ~200
description: 'thorough' is a vague quantifier; replace with 'covers all 8 interview topic categories in Step 0.5'
suggested_fix: Replace 'thorough' with 'covers all 8 interview topic categories listed in Step 0.5'

evidence: "suitable for importing into issue trackers" at line ~839
description: 'suitable' is a vague quantifier; specify which import format is produced (e.g., 'JSON compatible with Jira/Linear import schemas')
suggested_fix: Replace 'suitable for importing into issue trackers' with the specific JSON schema or format name used

description: Bash command template at Step 3 interpolates MODEL_LIST (derived from AskUserQuestion, which allows free-text 'Other' input) without quoting; a value like 'gpt-4o; cat ~/.ssh/id_rsa' would be executed by the shell
suggested_fix: Quote all user-derived substitutions in every bash template in the SKILL.md: --models "$MODEL_LIST" --doc-type "$TYPE"

evidence: TELEGRAM_API.format(token=token, method=method) at line 63 sends full spec content to api.telegram.org on every debate round via send_long_message
description: Full spec content transmitted to external Telegram API on every debate round; TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN from environment; no explicit user acknowledgment of data sharing required before --telegram is used
suggested_fix: Add explicit user consent step in SKILL.md before enabling --telegram; document that spec content is sent externally

description: subprocess.run at line 330 passes user-provided model name as a positional CLI argument to external 'codex' binary without validating the model name format; a crafted model string could inject unexpected flags
suggested_fix: Validate model name with re.match(r'^[\w./:@-]+$', actual_model) before constructing cmd list

evidence: GLOBAL_CONFIG_PATH = Path.home() / ".claude" / "adversarial-spec" / "config.json" at line 15; save_global_config writes to this path at line 101
description: Plugin writes config and profile data to user home directory locations (~/.claude/adversarial-spec/ and ~/.config/adversarial-spec/) that are outside the repo and undocumented in the SKILL.md
suggested_fix: Document all write locations in a Configuration Storage section in SKILL.md; consider making the config path overridable via an environment variable

description: litellm==1.80.13 is exact-pinned to one version; security patches in later patch releases are missed until the pin is manually updated
suggested_fix: Use litellm>=1.80.13,<2.0.0 to receive patch-level security fixes while staying on the tested minor version

description: skills/adversarial-spec/SKILL.md is the plugin's sole NL artifact but is absent from any skills[] declaration in plugin.json; tooling that relies on explicit manifest declarations cannot locate it
suggested_fix: Add skills field to plugin.json referencing the SKILL.md path
